title: Merge of Giuseppe Scrivano's UserNSRoot to podman 1.6.4-dev

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *qfunq* To complete the [pull request process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process), please assign **baude** You can assign the PR to them by writing `/assign @baude` in a comment when ready. The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Hi @qfunq. Thanks for your PR.

I'm waiting for a [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) member to verify that this patch is reasonable to test. If it is, they should reply with `/ok-to-test` on its own line. Until that is done, I will not automatically test new commits in this PR, but the usual testing commands by org members will still work. Regular contributors should [join the org](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) to skip this step.

Once the patch is verified, the new status will be reflected by the `ok-to-test` label.

I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
What's the intent here? These seem like major, invasive changes (the CreateContainer one in particular seems like it will break a lot).
we dropped the intermediate mounting namespace as it was very fragile.  I don't think we have to add it again.  Are you seeing any issue with user namespaces?

Hi Matthew,

Thanks so much, you are absolutely right, the CreateContainer code
contained dreadful bugs. While reviewing it I noticed something really
strange, that the issues I was having with the main branch of podman had
completely vanished.

I have pinned down the issue ... previously I was making the volumes using
podman create before issuing the podman -v call. The idea of the patch was
to be able to mount a pre-created volume, but on testing it now, it doesn't
work at doing that!

The end result is I will create volumes using a podman run command rather
than podman create, and delete the bugged branch.

Thanks for your help,

Jonathan














On Thu, Nov 14, 2019 at 2:10 AM Matthew Heon <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> What's the intent here? These seem like major, invasive changes (the
> CreateContainer one in particular seems like it will break a lot).
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/4509?email_source=notifications&email_token=ANXVXETUECTO72K2DKZ4NUTQTSXS3A5CNFSM4JNEHGPKYY3PNVWWK3TUL52HS4DFVREXG43VMVBW63LNMVXHJKTDN5WW2ZLOORPWSZGOEEAKQTI#issuecomment-553691213>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/ANXVXEU4N4WNEK5LRILRRQLQTSXS3ANCNFSM4JNEHGPA>
> .
>

I realised this is related to some usability issues in the main branch:

The call to makeAccessible in CreateContainer has no balancing chown back
to the original when the mounts are taken down, potentially leaving the
volume in a state other than it was created in.


If the chowning is done earlier, and a balancing unchown later, it's more
transparent, gaining associativity for volume based podman commands.



With these ideas, the following will work ...
podman --rm --uidmap 0:4096:4095 -v testvol:test:Z alpine cat hello >
/test/msg

podman --rm --uidmap 0: 8192:4095 -v testvol:test:Z alpine cat hello >
/test/msg


The volume commands are reduced to atomic actions, in the above case ::

podman create volume testvol //create the volume in the original users name
space

chown --uidmap 0:4096:4095  testvol    //make testvol accessible ...
reduces makeAccessible in CreateContainer to a nop.
podman run --rm --uidmap 0:4096:4095 -v testvol:/test:Z  alpine touch
/test/msg      ///do something with the volume
unchown --uidmap 0:4096:4095 testvol   //return the volume to the original
user

chown --uidmap 0:8192:4095 testvol
podman run --rm --uidmap 0:8192:4095 -v testvol:/test:Z  alpine   touch
/test/msg
unchown --uidmap 0: 8192 :4095 testvol

currently none of the 3 raw podman commands above will work together ...
which makes setting up volumes very context specific: i.e

# podman run --rm  --uidmap 0:8192:4095 -v testvol:/test:Z alpine touch
/test/foo
Error: error stat'ing file
'/var/lib/containers/storage/volumes/testvol/_data': Permission denied: OCI
runtime permission denied error ...

I think this is ginkgo complaining ... podman was launched as root, so the
permission to chown is permitted! I guess ginkgo is getting called before
makeAccessible?

Jonathan
